mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and imminent recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the unemployment threshold fails to predict direction with a log-odds ratio > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields a cumulative 60-day return below baseline in > 55% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising industrial production offsets rate-driven valuation compression by confirming resilient real output, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside the tightening regime, or 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of triggered cases during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% annualized confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDP growth rates, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to predict direction with a Sharpe ratio > 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a supply-constrained regime signals demand destruction rather than inventory normalization, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during supply-constrained periods (Rank IC < 0.02), or retail sales declines produce positive returns in > 65% of cases.
